documents.name: Anshita Bhardwaj
documents.title: M.S. Data Science @ ASU | Specializing in Explainable AI
documents.summary: Data Science graduate student with research experience in deep learning and NLP. Skilled in building end-to-end data pipelines, from extracting and validating large-scale datasets of over 1.5 million records to developing predictive models using LSTMs and Transformers.

children.text: I am a passionate Data Science graduate student at Arizona State University, specializing in Explainable AI and machine learning applications in healthcare. My research focuses on building transparent, fair, and responsible AI systems that can be trusted in critical decision-making scenarios.

With hands-on experience in both industry and research settings, I have developed expertise in end-to-end data pipelines, from extracting and validating large-scale datasets to deploying production-ready AI models. My work spans across natural language processing, computer vision, and time-series analysis.

I am particularly interested in the intersection of AI and healthcare, where I have worked on projects involving diabetic patient readmission prediction, COVID-19 spread modeling, and deepfake detection. My goal is to create AI systems that are not only accurate but also explainable, fair, and beneficial to society.

documents.title: LLM-Powered Agent for Enterprise Document Intelligence
documents.summary: An enterprise-grade RAG pipeline and conversational agent for querying large knowledge bases, achieving a 75% reduction in answer-time.

children.text: Enabled accurate, on-demand querying of a large knowledge base, as measured by 95% retrieval accuracy and a 75% reduction in answer-time, by building a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline with LangChain and a ChromaDB vector database.

Developed a conversational AI agent that synthesizes complex information, as measured by its ability to generate coherent, multi-source summaries, by implementing a RAG architecture that feeds retrieved context into an LLM using advanced prompt engineering.

documents.title: Responsible AI for Healthcare Risk Prediction
documents.summary: A predictive model and dashboard for patient readmission risk that focuses on transparency (XAI) and fairness (Bias Mitigation).

children.text: Improved clinical trust and model transparency by implementing a SHAP-based dashboard to provide feature-level explanations for individual patient risk predictions.
Reduced demographic bias in predictions by 40% while maintaining 98% of baseline model accuracy, by auditing the system with Fairlearn and retraining it with fairness constraints.

documents.title: End-to-End MLOps Pipeline for Real-Time Anomaly Detection
documents.summary: A production-grade MLOps pipeline for real-time anomaly detection using Kafka for streaming, Spark for processing, and Kubernetes for deployment.

children.text: Engineered a high-throughput streaming data pipeline capable of processing 10,000+ events per minute with sub-second latency, using Kafka for data ingestion and Apache Spark for real-time inference.
Achieved automated scaling and fault tolerance by deploying the containerized Spark application on a Kubernetes cluster, ensuring high availability.

documents.title: Full-Stack LLM Agent with a Dynamic Knowledge Base
documents.summary: A user-facing, full-stack AI application featuring a React frontend and a FastAPI backend, allowing users to both chat with an agent and dynamically update its knowledge base via document uploads.

children.text: Architected and deployed a full-stack, user-facing AI application, as measured by the successful integration of a React frontend with a Python FastAPI backend, achieving a sub-200ms API response time.
Enabled real-time knowledge base expansion, as measured by the development of a file upload endpoint that automatically processes, embeds, and integrates new documents into a vector database.

documents.title: OS Scheduler Pro: Visual Process Management
documents.summary: A Python-based desktop simulator using Tkinter and Matplotlib to visualize core OS scheduling algorithms like FCFS, SJF, and Round Robin.

children.text: Developed a Python-based Process Scheduling Simulator using Tkinter and Matplotlib to visualize algorithms like FCFS, SJF, Priority, and Round Robin.
Optimized the software to handle up to 1,000 processes with real-time Gantt chart visualization, reducing average waiting time by 20%.

documents.title: Sentiment Analysis in Twitter using LSTM Networks
documents.summary: Applied LSTM networks with GloVe embeddings to a 2.5M tweet dataset, achieving 91.02% validation accuracy.

children.text: Applied LSTM networks with GloVe embeddings to a 2.5M tweet dataset, achieving 91.02% validation accuracy by optimizing preprocessing for sequential input.
Outperformed baseline models like Naive Bayes and Decision Trees by over 20%, demonstrating improved classification precision through enhanced data cleaning and transformation.
Orchestrated the successful extraction of a high-resolution dataset, compiling 1.5 million records across 28 years by scripting an automated Python solution to bypass CDC portal query limits.
Enhanced a raw hazard dataset for feature engineering, increasing data granularity 200-fold by executing a data validation pipeline and performing root cause analysis.

achievements: Automated Python solution for CDC portal

children.text: Optimized production planning by 25%, reduced inventory costs by 10%, and improved on-time deliveries by 15% through advanced data analysis and forecasting models.
Used LSTM models to forecast demand for 50+ products across 3 plants, enhancing resource allocation and supply chain efficiency.
Improved predictive accuracy by 18% by integrating sales data with external economic factors, resulting in better demand forecasting and inventory management.

achievements: LSTM models for 50+ products across 3 plants

children.text: Developed a sentiment analysis tool using BERT and Transformers, achieving 92% classification accuracy across 10,000+ customer reviews.
Collaborated with engineering and marketing teams to implement keyword extraction using TF-IDF, leading to a 15% increase in keyword relevance.

achievements: Focus on Explainable AI and Healthcare Applications
Active in AI Ethics and Responsible AI Research

documents.title: Artificial Intelligence Hallucinations Fueling Deepfake Threats: Detection and Prevention Approaches
documents.abstract: This research presents a hallucination-aware deepfake classifier using the FaceForensics++ dataset, extracting and processing faces with MTCNN and training models in PyTorch. The study introduces a novel hallucination divergence metric that reveals significant distribution shifts between real and synthetic data.

children.text: Built a hallucination-aware deepfake classifier using the FaceForensics++ dataset, extracting and processing faces with MTCNN and training models in PyTorch.
Achieved an overall accuracy of 92.3% on deepfake classification using a CNN-based model with advanced preprocessing techniques.
Introduced a hallucination divergence metric that revealed a significant distribution shift between real and synthetic data, improving detection precision through comparative analysis.

keyFindings: 0.87 Pearson correlation between hallucinated and deepfake outputs

documents.title: Optimizing LSTM Hyperparameters with Genetic Algorithms
documents.abstract: This study demonstrates the effectiveness of genetic algorithms in optimizing LSTM hyperparameters on the IMDB dataset, achieving superior performance compared to traditional optimization methods while reducing computational time.

children.text: Tuned LSTM hyperparameters on the IMDB dataset using genetic algorithms (GA), achieving an AUC of 0.946 with optimized network architecture.
Reduced model convergence time by 35% and demonstrated GA's superior balance of computational efficiency and model performance compared to grid search and random search methods.

keyFindings: AUC of 0.946 on IMDB sentiment analysis
Superior efficiency compared to traditional methods
Optimal balance of performance and computational cost

documents.title: Reimagining CAPTCHA Security: A Case for Retiring Alphanumeric Text-Based CAPTCHAs
documents.abstract: This paper proposes replacing outdated alphanumeric CAPTCHA systems with a CNN-based model using RetinaNet, demonstrating superior security and user experience through advanced computer vision techniques.

children.text: Replaced outdated alphanumeric CAPTCHA systems with a CNN-based model using RetinaNet in Python, achieving 95% accuracy across a 50,000-image dataset.
Boosted dataset efficiency by 40% through advanced preprocessing techniques, improving both security and user experience compared to traditional text-based CAPTCHAs.

keyFindings: Superior security compared to text-based CAPTCHAs
Enhanced user experience with visual recognition

documents.title: Modeling the Spread of COVID-19 in Lombardy: Adaptation of the SEIQRS Mathematical Framework
documents.abstract: This study adapts the SEIQRS epidemiological model to forecast COVID-19 spread in Lombardy, Italy, integrating dynamic parameters like social distancing and testing rates for improved prediction accuracy.

children.text: Adapted the SEIQRS epidemiological model to forecast COVID-19 spread, integrating dynamic parameters like social distancing and testing rates for comprehensive pandemic modeling.
Enhanced prediction reliability using Profile Likelihood Fit, achieving a 30% accuracy boost compared to baseline epidemiological models.

documents.title: Unleashing the Power of LSTM Networks for Sentiment Analysis in Twitter
documents.abstract: This research applies LSTM networks with GloVe embeddings to a massive 2.5M tweet dataset, achieving superior sentiment classification performance compared to traditional machine learning approaches.

children.text: Applied LSTM networks with GloVe embeddings to a 2.5M tweet dataset, achieving 91.02% validation accuracy through optimized preprocessing for sequential input.
Outperformed baseline models like Naive Bayes and Decision Trees by over 20%, demonstrating improved classification precision through enhanced data cleaning and transformation techniques.
